title: Beau Is Afraid
logline: An extremely anxious man's journey to visit his mother becomes a surreal nightmare odyssey through urban decay, suburban dysfunction, forest communes, and maternal psychological torment.
antagonistOrConflict: Beau's domineering mother and his own crippling anxiety

actBreaks.description: Beau's urban nightmare ends when he's rescued by Grace and Roger after running naked into the street

validation.ruling: The police car scene effectively ends Act 1 by removing Beau from his urban nightmare and transitioning him toward the suburban family sanctuary. The ominous police behavior and radio chatter create uncertainty while structurally moving the story into its next phase.

actBreaks.description: Beau flees the suburban family after Toni's apparent suicide, entering the forest

validation.ruling: Scene #67 (Toni's death discovery) serves as both midpoint and Act 2 ending. This moment destroys Beau's temporary sanctuary and launches the forest survival sequence. It's a more significant structural break than #68, as it contains the actual reversal that transforms the story's trajectory.

actBreaks.description: Beau leaves the forest commune and arrives at his mother's house in Castlestone

validation.ruling: Beau's return to his hometown effectively transitions into the final act. The recognition from townspeople and his zombie-like state signal the approach to final revelations about his mother and their relationship.

The ocean ending provides resolution through apparent peace, until the cruise ship appearance suggests the cycle may continue. This ambiguous conclusion fits the screenplay's psychological horror structure.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: Each act represents a distinct psychological/physical realm: urban anxiety hell, suburban family dysfunction, forest commune cult, and maternal home confrontation. The structure mirrors a psychological journey through different manifestations of Beau's fears.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: Extremely dense pacing with very short scenes (1.4pp average). Act 1 is frantic and claustrophobic. Act 2 slows into suburban malaise. Act 3 becomes ritualistic and communal. Act 4 intensifies into final psychological confrontation. The brevity of scenes creates relentless momentum.

incitingIncident.description: Beau calls his unresponsive landlord, then his domineering mother who guilt-trips him about missing the flight and family obligations.

validation.ruling: Scene #17 is a perfect inciting incident. Beau's call to his mother about the stolen keys launches the central story conflict. Her suspicious reaction ('What is this, Beau?') and the missed flight create the driving tension that propels the entire narrative. This moment transforms a simple logistics problem into a psychological crisis that defines the protagonist's journey.

midpoint.description: Grace discovers Toni's dead body with blue coating around her mouth and face locked in a choked contortion. Beau frantically insists it's not what it looks like while Grace desperately tries to revive her daughter.

validation.ruling: Scene #67 is the true midpoint - Toni's death fundamentally transforms the story. While #68 shows Beau fleeing, #67 contains the actual reversal: Grace discovers her daughter's corpse and Beau becomes a suspected killer. This death shatters the sanctuary of the surrogate family and forces Beau back into his nightmare journey. The structural shift from safety to danger is more significant than the physical act of running.

climax.description: Beau finds his supposedly dead mother alive in the living room as muscular young men carry out Elaine's corpse, revealing the entire scenario was orchestrated

validation.ruling: Scene #112 delivers the ultimate confrontation - Beau's mother reveals she faked her death and orchestrated his entire ordeal. This revelation recontextualizes the entire narrative as psychological manipulation. The appearance of the muscular men disposing of Elaine's corpse shows the extent of the mother's control and deception. This is the climactic unveiling of the story's central truth.

scenes.heading: INT. WAITING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Beau sits anxiously in a therapist's waiting room, displaying childlike innocence while surrounded by surveillance cameras and disturbing magazines.

scenes.heading: INT. THERAPIST’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: In therapy, Beau discusses his upcoming visit to his mother for his father's death anniversary, expressing fear and nervousness about seeing her.

scenes.heading: EXT. BUILDING - EVNING
scenes.summary: Leaving therapy, Beau encounters a crowd filming a suicidal man on a rooftop, with people trying to encourage him to jump.

scenes.heading: INT. PHARMACY - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: At the pharmacy, Beau witnesses homeless junkies stealing drugs while the pharmacist ignores the theft, heightening his anxiety.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARK - EVENING
scenes.summary: Beau finds brief peace feeding ducks at a park lake, a moment of calm in his chaotic day.

scenes.heading: INT. SUBWAY - EVENING
scenes.summary: On the subway, Beau observes a father-son relationship with longing, then becomes uncomfortable when a woman stares at him lustfully.

scenes.heading: EXT. APARTMENT BUILDING - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau hurriedly enters his run-down apartment building surrounded by homeless encampments, desperately avoiding interaction.

scenes.heading: INT. ELEVATOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The elevator malfunctions with all buttons lighting up, reflecting the building's deteriorated state.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Sandy tells Beau about a neighbor who died from a brown recluse bite, triggering a violent flashback of the medical emergency.

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S APARTMENT - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Beau enters his hazard-proofed apartment, unaware that a deadly brown recluse spider lurks in the darkness.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: In his bedroom, Beau looks at mementos including a high school photo with Elaine while disturbing news plays on TV.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - LATER THAT NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau tries to sleep but is kept awake by city noise and neighbors playing loud music, forcing him to count sheep.

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S BEDROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Beau oversleeps until 3 PM due to the noise, realizing he's missed his flight time and frantically begins getting ready.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Beau frantically brushes his teeth and packs toiletries, rushing to make up for lost time.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Grabbing his plane ticket, Beau rushes out but forgets something, leaving his key in the door while retrieving floss.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau discovers his keys and suitcase have been stolen from his door, and a strange man tells him he's 'fucked.'

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S APARTMENT - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Beau calls his unresponsive landlord, then his domineering mother who guilt-trips him about missing the flight and family obligations.
scenes.turningPointNote: First major crisis - keys stolen, mother's manipulation begins

scenes.heading: INT. BUILDING LOBBY - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Locked out, Beau props the building door open with a phone book and prepares to venture into the dangerous homeless crowd.

scenes.heading: EXT. APARTMENT BUILDING - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau sprints through the homeless encampment to reach the convenience store, dodging threatening individuals.

scenes.heading: INT. CONVENIENCE STORE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: At the store, Beau's credit card is declined and he notices someone has discovered his door prop, threatening his return.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau is locked out as homeless people invade his apartment building, and the cashier refuses to help him call for assistance.
scenes.turningPointNote: Complete loss of home and safety - homeless invasion begins

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - LATER THAT NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau climbs a tree to spy on his apartment, watching homeless people destroy his home and abuse his belongings through the night.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - NEXT MORNING
scenes.summary: Beau wakes up in the tree to find his apartment seemingly empty, cautiously approaching the building with its shattered front door.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - MORNING
scenes.summary: Beau finds a dead homeless man at his door, killed by the brown recluse spider, which then scampers into his apartment.

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S APARTMENT - MORNING
scenes.summary: Beau attempts to kill the brown recluse with his TV but fails, managing to trap it outside and lock his door.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau surveys his destroyed kitchen, seeing the complete devastation left by the homeless invasion.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The bedroom shows evidence of drug abuse, sex, and violence from the homeless occupation.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau carefully checks the bathroom to ensure it's empty and safe before proceeding.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Beau calls a locksmith who can only come later that afternoon, extending his vulnerable situation.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: While booking new flights, Beau tries calling his mother but gets a wrong number, suggesting something has happened to her.
scenes.turningPointNote: First hint that mother may be dead or unreachable

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Beau wakes up on his couch calling for his mother, still disoriented and seeking comfort.

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Beau looks through a photo album of memories with his mother, whose face remains mysteriously obscured in every picture.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Taking a bath for comfort, Beau blames himself for not being there, when mysterious sounds from above terrify him.

scenes.heading: EXT. APARTMENT BUILDING - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau runs naked from his building to a police officer, desperately seeking help but appearing deranged.

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE CAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The corrupt policeman takes photos of naked Beau, drives past the police station, and delivers him to violent criminals who stab him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Beau is betrayed by authority and nearly killed - major trauma

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - NIGHT/CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau wakes up bandaged in a bright bedroom, tended to by the saintly Grace who has been caring for him for two days.
scenes.turningPointNote: Introduction of Grace and Roger - sanctuary after violence

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - THE NEXT MORNING
scenes.summary: Grace and Roger sit by Beau's bed as he wakes up, beginning to show him kindness and family warmth he's never experienced.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - MORNING
scenes.summary: At breakfast, Beau explains his bizarre ordeal to Roger and Grace, who listen with sympathy and understanding.

scenes.heading: INT. STUDY - DAY
scenes.summary: Beau calls his mother's attorney and learns she has died, with the lawyer callously revealing she died the day Beau was supposed to visit.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mother's death confirmed - Beau's central relationship and source of guilt/trauma revealed

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Beau does puzzles with Grace while wrestling with guilt over his mother's death, as troubled teen Toni disrupts the family peace.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAWN - DAY
scenes.summary: Beau calls his therapist for help processing his mother's death and guilt, seeking professional guidance in his crisis.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Grace sets up therapy for Beau with her friend Judy, showing continued care and support for his healing.

scenes.heading: INT. TONI’S BEDROOM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Toni secretly watches Beau being comforted by her family while stealing her mother's prescription pills.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR (MOVING) - DAY
scenes.summary: Grace drives Beau to therapy, asking about his love life and encouraging him to open up about high school sweetheart Elaine.

scenes.heading: INT. BUILDING - DAY
scenes.summary: Grace introduces Beau to Jeeves, a traumatized POW security guard, showing Beau he's not alone in his trauma.

scenes.heading: INT. WAITING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Beau meets trauma therapist Judy who warmly welcomes him for his first session.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Judy explains the nervous system and trauma response to Beau, offering professional insight into his anxiety condition.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR (MOVING) - DAY
scenes.summary: After therapy, Grace tells Beau that Judy likes him and wants to see him twice weekly, building his confidence.

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: At home, Toni confronts Beau about 'enjoying himself' and reveals he's not the first 'charity case' the family has helped.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR (MOVING) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Toni takes Beau out with her friend Liz, pressuring him to smoke drugs to prove his trustworthiness, creating new tension.
scenes.turningPointNote: Toni's manipulation threatens Beau's recovery and safety with Grace's family

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau sits stoned in the desert, watching Toni and Liz take turns wrapping their mouths around a car's tailpipe while the other revs the engine, forcing them to inhale toxic exhaust fumes.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR (MOVING) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Toni, Beau, and Liz drive back from the desert in complete silence after their disturbing exhaust inhalation ritual.

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Returning to the dark house where Roger and Grace sleep, Toni leads Beau inside and sarcastically asks what he's waiting for as they head to the bedroom.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In pajamas, Toni aggressively forces the reluctant Beau to take her bed despite his protests, tucking him in with threatening hospitality while he clearly wants to sleep on the floor.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - MORNING
scenes.summary: At breakfast, a haggard-looking Beau and Toni sit with Grace, who asks about their night. Under Toni's pressure, Beau reluctantly claims they had a good time.

scenes.heading: INT. BUILDING - DAY
scenes.summary: Grace walks Beau through the building lobby again, passing Jeeves who ominously follows them with his eyes without moving his head.

scenes.heading: INT. JUDY’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: In therapy, Judy guides Beau through a body relaxation exercise, helping him identify tension in his neck, shoulders, back, and teeth, encouraging him to use metaphors to describe his feelings.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR (MOVING) - EVENING
scenes.summary: While Grace drives, Beau struggles to ask about visiting his mother in Castlestone. Grace immediately understands and enthusiastically agrees to accompany him, making the conversation easy for him.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau settles on the couch for the night, but Toni angrily confronts him, insisting he must sleep in her bed per her parents' instructions and threatening to count to three.

scenes.heading: INT. GRACE’S & ROGER’S BEDROOM - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Toni wakes her parents to complain about Beau sleeping on the couch instead of her bed. Roger calls her hypocritical and threatens to get cross when she calls his bluff.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Toni returns to whisper a disturbing threat in Beau's ear about making him breathe toxic rubber cement fumes while he sleeps. Beau tries to calm himself by practicing the body awareness technique.

scenes.heading: INT. TONI’S BEDROOM = SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Toni storms into her bedroom, slams the door, and curls up in her sleeping bag in concentrated rage, cursing Beau for coming into her home.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau lies gripping his blanket, listening to Toni's muffled angry swearing through the wall, anticipating the consequences of her hatred for him.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NEXT DAY
scenes.summary: The next day, Beau practices body awareness meditation when Roger suddenly appears demanding he grill tri-tip. As Beau puts on shoes, Toni confronts him aggressively.

scenes.heading: INT. GARAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: In the garage, Toni forces Beau to get high with her despite his protests about stomach pain and grilling duties. She accuses him of stealing her parents and declares she doesn't believe his mother was murdered, devastating him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Toni's accusation about his mother being murdered hits Beau hard, escalating their conflict to a breaking point

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Grace hears the commotion from the garage while chopping carrots in the kitchen, including the muffled sounds of Beau pleading with Toni to stop, prompting her to rush toward the garage.

scenes.heading: INT. GARAGE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Grace discovers Toni's dead body with blue coating around her mouth and face locked in a choked contortion. Beau frantically insists it's not what it looks like while Grace desperately tries to revive her daughter.
scenes.turningPointNote: Toni's death is a major catastrophic turning point that will drive Beau to flee

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau tears into the forest in a hyperventilating panic, running furiously as his anxiety mounts, unable to stop even if he wanted to.
scenes.turningPointNote: Beau's flight into the forest begins his next major journey and escape from the family

scenes.heading: EXT. HOUSE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: From the forest's perspective, Roger's bone-chilling wails of anguish echo from the distant house as he discovers his daughter's death, startling birds from the trees.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Beau continues sprinting through the woods, crying hysterically until he crashes face-first into a tree branch and falls unconscious. He dreams of his mother promising they'll never leave each other, making a double pinky swear.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - EVENING
scenes.summary: Beau awakens in the forest at evening after being unconscious for a day. Dehydrated, he drinks from a pond only to realize it's filthy with dead fish floating in it, then hears a twig snap nearby.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Michael, a camouflaged man, leads Beau to a forest clearing with a giant tent and impressive treehouse colony called 'Orphans of the Forest.' A confident naked man greets Beau enthusiastically with a big hug.
scenes.turningPointNote: Beau's discovery of the Orphans colony begins a major new phase of his journey

scenes.heading: INT. TENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the tent at night, Beau lies preoccupied in his sleeping bag while others sleep, staring at the ceiling and telling himself his mother was murdered.

scenes.heading: EXT. DINING SPACE - THE NEXT MORNING
scenes.summary: At breakfast, twenty Orphans sit in meditation before eating. Brennan leads the meal while Billy explains the routine to Beau, who self-consciously begins serving himself from the vegetarian feast.

scenes.heading: EXT. TREEHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Beau watches everyone work on colony projects while Brennan paces, giving mindful instructions about observing the forest and feeling their bodies. When offered a task, Beau agrees to help.

scenes.heading: EXT. GARDEN - DAY
scenes.summary: While pulling weeds with Sunflower in the garden, Beau explains he came here running away. When Brennan calls out to feel feet on the ground, Beau experiences deja vu and the earth trembles beneath him.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Jeeves, wearing black hunting gear and a necklace of yellow teeth, stomps purposefully down a street carrying Beau's photo with his face circled in red, then turns toward the forest as thunder strikes.

scenes.heading: INT. TONI’S BEDROOM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: During a torrential downpour, Grace sits weeping on Toni's bed holding her daughter's photo, blaming herself for bringing Beau into their home, then rage-filled, calls for Jeeves to rip him apart.

scenes.heading: INT. VITAMIN SHACK - DAY
scenes.summary: The Orphans queue for daily vitamin injections from a naked Aboriginal witch doctor. Brennan leads reluctant Beau to the front, assuring him they're essential vitamins despite Beau's unease.

scenes.heading: INT. GIANT TENT - DAY
scenes.summary: In the tent while changing into hemp meditation robes, a bearded Orphan is astonished by Beau's unusually large testicles, dramatically pointing them out to everyone's attention.

scenes.heading: INT. MEDITATION SHACK - DAY
scenes.summary: During meditation, Brennan tries to help anxious Beau with his grief, but Beau desperately wants to discuss getting to Castlestone for his murdered mother. Brennan insists he close his eyes and be present.

scenes.heading: INT. YESEKOV’S SHACK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Everyone quietly gathers around dying Yesekov's bed in hushed awe. Billy explains to Beau that it's beautiful when someone passes, as the body is a prison for the soul, but the experience disturbs Beau.

scenes.heading: INT. MEDITATION SHACK - EVENING
scenes.summary: At evening meditation, everyone sits cross-legged but appears too shaken by Yesekov's death to properly meditate, with several people keeping their eyes open and looking around skeptically.

scenes.heading: EXT. DINING SPACE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: During dinner at the picnic table, awkward silence pervades as Beau sits between shaken Orphans, anxiously eyeing an escape route through the woods while voices in his head say he needs to get home.

scenes.heading: INT. MAIN TREEHOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the main treehouse, Beau is socializing with laughing Orphans when Brennan calls him to lie on a couch under a spotlight. Using a strange machine, Brennan interrogates Beau about his life, parents, and sexuality while others watch.
scenes.turningPointNote: The interrogation scene reveals Beau's deepest fears and trauma in front of the group, marking a crucial violation of his privacy

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jeeves marches through the forest with nightmare-like determination until confronting a grizzly bear. After a tense face-off, he ends up confidently riding the bear up a hill.

scenes.heading: INT. GIANT TENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the tent at night, Beau lies awake while an Orphan throws knives at a photo of George Clooney, claiming the actor pushed his father in front of a train. Others share similar celebrity grievances.

scenes.heading: INT. GIANT TENT - NGIHT
scenes.summary: Brennan quietly enters the tent where Beau sleeps, waking Charles to show him something outside. Beau notices Billy's forest map by his empty sleeping bag and secretly pockets it.

scenes.heading: EXT. GIANT TENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Following Brennan outside, Charles and Beau discover the other Orphans in ritualistic face-paint around a fire, with an older man tied to a ceremonial tree, clearly part of some disturbing ritual.

scenes.heading: INT. GIANT TENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau lies extremely awake battling a panic attack while others sleep. When he tries to leave for the bathroom, Billy questions why he won't use the hole in the tent, making escape difficult.

scenes.heading: EXT. GIANT TENT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau carefully exits the tent and tries to escape, passing Charles' father's dead body tied to the tree. An alarm sounds, a spotlight hits him, and Brennan confronts him over the speaker, accusing him of never trying.
scenes.turningPointNote: Beau's failed escape attempt and Brennan's confrontation marks the climax of his time with the Orphans

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau runs through the woods with unprecedented ferocity, his brain as addled as his legs are fast, crashing through branches and obstacles with desperate determination to escape.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - NEXT DAY
scenes.summary: After running all night, an exhausted Beau reaches a pond and pulls out Billy's stolen map, discovering the nearest town is only one mile away from his current location.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Emerging from bushes onto a road, Beau finds a bus stop with a route map showing Castlestone as a destination. A bus arrives and its doors swing open for him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Beau finally finds transportation to return home, marking his transition back toward his mother

scenes.heading: INT. BUS (MOVING) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau sits in the back of a mostly empty bus as it drives quietly through the night toward his destination.

scenes.heading: EXT. CASTLESTONE STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: A haggard Beau emerges from the bus in beautiful Castlestone, showing the toll of his recent traumas. Local residents including a butcher and homeless man recognize him and stare as he passes zombie-like.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEAU’S MOTHER’S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Beau approaches his mother's mailbox and sentimentally touches it before looking up the long driveway at her large, beautiful white and blue beach house beside a glittering lake.

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S MOTHER’S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Entering his mother's house, Beau walks through sunlit rooms filled with dust particles and advertising standees featuring his mother promoting prescription medications like Paxim.
scenes.turningPointNote: Beau's return to his mother's house begins the final act of confronting his origins

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Walking the hallway adorned with photos of his childhood, Beau sees images of being home-schooled, cooking with mother, Halloween costumes, and notably a sequence of him being attacked by bees while his mother tends to his stings.

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S MOTHER’S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: In his mother's bedroom, Beau sits overwhelmed on her made bed, smelling a strand of her hair from the pillow. He finds her shelves stacked with journals and begins reading one from 1975 about her swelling heart of love.
scenes.turningPointNote: Beau discovering his mother's journals represents the beginning of uncovering the truth about their relationship and his past

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S MOTHER’S HOUSE - DAY - FLASHBACK
scenes.summary: Flashback reveals young Beau's mother writing in her journal about lying to 8-year-old Beau, telling him his father died during conception on their honeymoon instead of the truth
scenes.turningPointNote: Major revelation about the foundational lie that shaped Beau's entire understanding of his origins

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S MOTHER’S BEDROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Present day Beau closes the journal, processing the shocking truth with confused rage and discovers a door marked 'MAMA'S WORKSHOP'

scenes.heading: INT. MAMA’S WORKSHOP - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau enters his mother's workshop filled with advertising materials and discovers a board for 'MAMA WILMINGTON'S ULTIMATE SURVEILLANCE' boasting about observing her own son for 23 years without detection
scenes.turningPointNote: Reveals the full extent of his mother's surveillance and manipulation throughout his entire adult life

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau aggressively scrubs himself in the shower and shaves angrily in front of the mirror

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Clean-shaven but bloodied from shaving, Beau searches a phonebook and calls Elaine Bray, learning from her mother that she's probably at a local bar

scenes.heading: INT. SQUIRREL DEN BAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At the seedy Squirrel Den Bar, Beau awkwardly approaches the beautiful Elaine who doesn't recognize him but is friendly, and they begin talking

scenes.heading: INT. ELAINE’S CAR (MOVING) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elaine drives Beau home while touching his thigh, making cryptic comments and veering dangerously into the median before Beau helps steer back

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S MOTHER’S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau and Elaine enter his mother's house where she flips on lights and makes suggestive comments about taking off shoes and lighting candles

scenes.heading: INT. BEAU’S MOTHER’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In his mother's bedroom, Elaine lights candles and tells Beau to go to the bathroom so she can get ready

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Beau stares at himself in the bathroom mirror and gives himself a pep talk, affirming 'You're a man'

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Beau and Elaine have sex in his mother's bed, with Beau extremely tense and inexperienced, culminating in his apparent climax and immediate death
scenes.turningPointNote: Beau's first and final sexual experience results in his death, fulfilling his mother's prophecy

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Beau finds his supposedly dead mother alive in the living room as muscular young men carry out Elaine's corpse, revealing the entire scenario was orchestrated
scenes.turningPointNote: Ultimate revelation that his mother faked her death and orchestrated Beau's sexual encounter and Elaine's murder

scenes.heading: INT. SECRET PUNISHMENT ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Locked in a secret punishment room filled with old furniture and toys, Beau discovers a giant, trembling mass of wrinkled flesh making raspy breathing sounds

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau collapses hysterically as his mother reveals the grotesque creature is his father, while the therapist laughs maniacally at Beau's horror and disbelief
scenes.turningPointNote: The ultimate horror - discovering his father's true monstrous form and the complete cruelty of his mother's deception

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Beau walks numbly downstairs past the bullet-ridden bodies of his mother's two young assistants lying in pools of blood

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: In the kitchen, Beau finds both his mother's keys and his own stolen keys on the counter as a fire ignites upstairs

scenes.heading: EXT. BEAU’S MOM’S HOUSE - NIGHT/MORNING
scenes.summary: Beau exits the burning house at sunrise, passes Brennan's corpse, and finds the small man in a parka now dressed as a chauffeur sleeping in his mother's car before walking to the lake

scenes.heading: EXT. LAKE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Beau sits in a sailing boat approaching a fork with two tunnels marked 'TOWN' and '???' and chooses the mysterious path
scenes.turningPointNote: Beau makes his final choice to embrace the unknown rather than return to conventional life

scenes.heading: EXT. OCEAN - DAY
scenes.summary: Beau's boat reaches the open ocean where he finds supplies including water, food, binoculars, fishing rod, bird watching book, and War & Peace as he sits peacefully in the sun
scenes.turningPointNote: Final scene suggests Beau may have found peace or death in isolation, ending his lifelong anxiety
